Highlight: # Highlight: 'Yes' marks key summary plots to feature prominently.
# Truth Source: External data source to compare against. Values: eia (EIA state-level data)

# Quantity: Energy quantity or metric to plot. 'Number of dwelling units' is a housing stock count. 'All Enduses' compares all fuel/enduse combinations together. Others are specific fuel+enduse combos (e.g. 'Electricity Space Cooling').

# Metric: What is being measured. 'total annual consumption' = stock-wide sum over a year. 'average monthly consumption' = per-unit mean by month. 'annual consumption distribution' = per-unit quartile box plots. 'enduse penetration' = % of units with non-zero consumption. LRD metrics use temporal resolutions: 'average daily consumption' (day of year)
Quantity: 'hourly consumption vs temperature' (consumption binned by outdoor temperature).

Highlight: # Coverage: Population scope. 'all units' / 'all occupied units' = every unit in the stock. 'units with non-zero consumption' / 'occupied units with non-zero consumption' = only units that actually use that fuel (relevant for natural gas
# Group By: Dimension to group/facet the data. 'state' and 'utility' produce tilemap layouts. Others (census division recs
Coverage: heating fuel) produce grouped bar charts.

Highlight: # Main Visualization: Primary chart type. Values: tilemap bar plot (state/utility map + bars)
Main Visualization: daily load shape matrix (hour × month heatmap)

Highlight: # Extra Visualization: Secondary chart shown alongside the main visualization. 'difference view' shows percent difference between ResStock and the truth source. 'temperature count' shows the distribution of temperature observations. Blank means no extra visualization.
